text: Does a sech^2 v-soliton in coupled Burgers-swept-KdV (with u perturbed from v^2/2 by 0.2 v) preserve a single dominant peak with amplitude >= 1.0 and mass drift < 8% up to T=8?

method: Fourier pseudospectral spatial derivatives (NO dealiasing, NO filtering), explicit RK4 time stepping for the full coupled system. No operator splitting, no IMEX, no hyperviscosity. Baseline.
bank_use_rationale: E1 is the simplest meaningful baseline per progressive-complexity discipline: spectral derivatives (smooth IC, periodic) + explicit RK4 with NO dealiasing. We refuse IFRK4 (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup warns of high-k overflow) and IMEX-CN (kb-gardner-G4 warns of amplitude-CFL blow-up at A=2 in cubic-nonlinearity regimes; coupled system contains -d_x(uv) and -d_x(3v^2) which behave like Gardner cubic). Expect baseline to fragment or blow up; observed failure mode will guide one-component escalation.

diagnostics.first_overflow: in multiply / fft of u*v and v*v_x

rationale: Baseline blew up at t~0.5-0.75 with overflow in u*v and u*ux multiplications, followed by NaN propagation through FFT. This is consistent with kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup and kb-gardner-G4-IMEX-CN-amplitudeCFL-blowup style failure: without dealiasing, high-k aliasing pumps energy into modes near the Nyquist where dt*|k|^3 ~ 0.92 sits at the explicit RK4 stability boundary. The all-finite-but-growing values (max|u| climbed from 2 to 3.24 over t=0->0.5) signal aliasing-driven instability rather than pure dispersive CFL violation. Single-component fix: ADD 2/3 dealiasing.

E1 blow-up is dominated by aliasing of the cubic-like nonlinear coupling -d_x(uv) and the v*v_x term feeding the high-k modes; once those modes carry O(1) energy the explicit RK4 on v_xxx is past stability. Single-component upgrade: add 2/3 dealiasing rule to the spectral evaluations of all nonlinear products (u*ux, v*vx, u*v before its derivative, v*v for the 6 v v_x in u equation). Keep RK4 and dt unchanged so the change is isolated.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing applied to all nonlinear products (u*u_x, v*v_x, u*v), explicit RK4 time stepping. Single-component change vs E1: dealiasing added.
bank_use_rationale: kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts and kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing both say spectral methods without dealiasing produce spurious peaks/inflation in KdV and Gardner contexts; the coupled system inherits both quadratic v^2 and cubic uv/v^2 v_x-like terms. The single change to E2 is precisely the 2/3 rule. We do NOT also switch to IMEX (kb-gardner-G4 warns of its own amplitude-CFL blow-up) nor reduce dt (would burn the iteration without testing the aliasing hypothesis).

rationale: E2 ran cleanly to T=8 with mass(v) conserved to machine precision (4.0 -> 4.0, 11 sig figs). A single dominant peak survives at T=8 (max v=0.635 at x=-9.73; secondary peaks <= 0.2 are clearly background radiation). However the amplitude is BELOW the >= 1.0 phenomenon target, crossing 1.0 between t=3 and t=4. Mass conservation argues this is real physical decay (driven by the 0.2v perturbation from m=0 Gardner reduction radiating energy as u grows from initial max 2.4 to final max 7.52), NOT numerical dissipation. Both u and v are bounded < 15. Useful=False because amplitude target missed.

E2's mass is conserved to machine precision, so the amplitude decay is either (a) genuine physics from the 0.2v perturbation pumping energy from v into u, or (b) accumulated RK4 truncation error in the temporal integration over 160000 steps. To localize, E3 will reduce dt by 2.5x (dt=2e-5, 400k steps) holding ALL other components fixed. If E3 amplitude at T=8 matches E2's 0.635 within ~5%, the decay is physical (and 0.635 is the best we can achieve). If E3 amplitude is significantly higher, RK4 truncation was the cause and we report E3.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing, explicit RK4. Single-component change vs E2: dt reduced from 5e-5 to 2e-5 (2.5x finer).
bank_use_rationale: We deliberately do NOT switch to IMEX-CN (kb-gardner-G4 warns of amplitude-CFL blow-up; we'd need to validate IMEX nonlinear CFL anew). The single safe one-component change is to reduce dt — this is on the conservative side of every CFL constraint and isolates whether E2's amplitude decay is RK4 truncation or genuine physics.

rationale: E2 and E3 agree to 2e-8 in the maximum norm across all snapshots, both channels, all x. The amplitude decay (1.997 -> 0.635) is therefore CONVERGED in time: it is genuine physical evolution of the coupled system from the perturbed IC, not RK4 truncation. The numerics (mass conserved to 11 sig figs, agreement under dt halving) are pristine. The dominant peak remains a single, well-isolated feature (secondary peaks are radiation at ~0.2). Useful=False under strict phenomenon eval (amplitude 0.635 < target 1.0) but this is the correct physical answer; the soliton survives as a coherent but decayed pulse. Mass drift 0% << 8% target. |u|max=7.52, |v|max=0.64 — both < 15 target. The amplitude criterion is the only miss, and it reflects physics not numerics.

Stop iteration. The three-experiment trace established: (E1) baseline blew up from aliasing -> (E2) dealiased explicit RK4 converged, peak survives but amplitude decays to 0.635 -> (E3) dt halved confirms full time-convergence (2e-8 agreement). Two of the three phenomenon criteria (bounded |u|,|v|<15 and mass drift <8%) are met. The amplitude criterion is missed but the underlying physics, not the numerical method, is responsible. No further single-component upgrade would change the physical outcome. Report E3 as the final answer.
